ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Duck Rice as a composite Singaporean dish. Mapped to 'duck dish' as the closest defensible parent class. Composite rice-and-duck dish not individually represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Duck meat dishes — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for a composite braised/roasted duck with rice preparation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Duck Rice (Singaporean/Malaysian composite dish). A0FKP (duck meat dishes) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Duck Rice as a culturally specific composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard hawker serving of Duck Rice consists of approximately 400g, comprising a portion of braised or roasted duck pieces served over yam rice or plain white rice, with dark gravy poured over and optional side dishes such as braised egg and tofu.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrition facts of common Singaporean rice dishes
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) data for common Singaporean rice dishes. Duck Rice is a recognized hawker dish in the HPB food composition database. Values represent a composite of braised/roasted duck with yam rice and dark gravy. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central duck meat and rice composition data as HPB does not separately publish saturated fat for this dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous rice dish entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Duck Rice as a composite Singaporean hawker dish. GI of 62 is estimated based on the dominant carbohydrate component: yam rice (rice cooked with yam, dried shrimp, and dark soy sauce). Yam has a GI of approximately 51 (University of Sydney GI Database) and white rice approximately 72; the composite yam rice is estimated at GI ~60–65. Protein and fat from duck further moderate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 58.0) / 100 = 35.96, rounded to 35.7. Carbohydrate per 400g serving = 14.5 × 4 = 58.0g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce-based braising liquid and dark gravy
hypertension.guidance: A 400g serving of Duck Rice delivers approximately 1280mg sodium, representing over 55% of the recommended daily limit of 2000mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced gravy, remove duck skin, and limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 62) with significant carbohydrate load from rice component
type2_diabetes.guidance: Duck Rice has a medium GI of approximately 62; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose yam rice over plain white rice for its higher fibre content, control portion size to limit carbohydrate intake to under 45–60g per meal, and be mindful of added sugar in the braising sauce.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from duck skin and elevated sodium from soy-based gravy
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Duck Rice contains moderate saturated fat (estimated 2.8g per 100g) and high sodium (320mg per 100g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should remove duck skin to reduce saturated fat intake, limit consumption of the dark gravy, and prefer a smaller portion with additional vegetables to improve the overall dietary profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Duck provides zinc and selenium; herbal braising stock contains anti-inflammatory spices
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Duck Rice provides zinc (1.2mg/100g) and selenium (5.5mcg/100g) which support immune function; the herbal braising stock with star anise and galangal has mild anti-inflammatory properties, though high dark meat intake should be balanced with vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Duck Rice (鸭饭, Ya Fan) is a beloved hawker dish originating from Chinese immigrant communities in Singapore and Malaysia, with roots in Teochew and Hokkien culinary traditions. The dish exists in two primary forms: Teochew-style braised duck, where the bird is slow-cooked in a fragrant 'lor' master stock of soy sauce, star anise, cinnamon, galangal, and five-spice until deeply flavoured and tender; and Hokkien-style roasted duck, which yields a crispier, lacquered skin. Both versions are served over yam rice — rice steamed with diced yam, dried shrimp, and dark soy sauce — or plain white rice, and are generously topped with a thick, savoury dark gravy. Common accompaniments include braised hard-boiled eggs, braised tofu, and a clear herbal soup, with a side of chilli-vinegar dipping sauce. Nutritionally, a 400g serving provides approximately 700 kcal, 32.8g protein, 38g fat, and 58g carbohydrate. The dish is moderate in glycemic index (GI ~62) due to the yam component, which contributes dietary fibre and slows glucose absorption compared to plain white rice. Sodium content is high (320mg/100g; ~1280mg per serving) due to the soy-based braising liquid, making it a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Duck is a good source of B vitamins, zinc, and selenium. Clinically, portion control and gravy reduction are the primary dietary modifications recommended for individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular risk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Duck Rice Singapore?
Is Duck Rice suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from Health Promotion Board Singapore data for common Singaporean rice dishes, which is an approved primary regional source. Duck Rice is a recognized hawker dish in the HPB database. Saturated fat was estimated from USDA FoodData Central duck and rice composition data as HPB does not separately publish saturated fat breakdown for this composite dish. GI is an analogous estimate based on yam rice and white rice GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database, as no direct GI measurement for Duck Rice exists in the published literature. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.8g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central duck meat (USDA FDC ID 782423, roasted duck) and cooked white rice composition, as HPB does not separately publish saturated fat for Duck Rice. This is documented as an adapted source match. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g), cholesterol_mg, and moisture data (water_g) for this composite Singaporean hawker preparation are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore food composition database or USDA FoodData Central for Duck Rice as a composite dish as of 2024. The HPB database reports total fat only for this dish category; USDA FoodData Central does not contain a matching composite entry for Singaporean Duck Rice with yam rice and braising gravy. These fields are therefore set to -1 to indicate data not available from any approved source, rather than confirmed zero values. 3. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Duck Rice exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2024. The estimate is based on yam rice (GI ~60–65) as the dominant carbohydrate source, moderated by duck protein and fat. 4. Halal status is marked false as traditional Duck Rice preparations may use pork-derived ingredients in the braising stock at some hawker stalls; halal-certified versions exist but are not universal. 5. The input record contained a duplicate portion entry (two identical 400g entries); deduplicated to a single canonical serving of 400g. 6. Ontology: FoodOn URI mapped to 'duck dish' as the closest defensible parent; FoodEx2 code A0FKP used as the closest duck meat dish anchor. No SNOMED CT code identified for this culturally specific composite dish.
